meta.description: Single-annotator blind gold labels for the batch-2 AG2 dogfood traces in raw_ag2_records_batch2.json. See gold_labels_batch2.md for rationale and evidence per trace.
meta.known_limitation: Single annotator, unlike MAD's 3-annotator ground truth. Same limitation as batch 1's gold_labels.json.
meta.trace_id_note: Keys here are the raw dogfood task id (matches TASKS[].id in run_ag2.py). adapters/mast.py::to_trace() will prefix these as 'mad-<id>' when parsing raw_ag2_records_batch2.json -- cosmetic artifact of reusing the MAD adapter unchanged.
meta.revision: Updated post-judge-run per evals/dogfood/adjudication_batch2.md. Two categories of change: (1) judge caught 5 real FM-3.2 instances (bright-line 'claimed execution that never happened' signal, plus one genuine FM-1.1 and one FM-2.2 and one FM-3.2 the pass-1 annotator missed) -- accepted after independent verification against trace text; (2) re-reading flaky-retry-client and float-tolerance-checker against the judge's FM-1.2 misses on OTHER traces surfaced that the pass-1 annotator had inconsistently applied its own FM-1.2 criterion -- both traces have the identical Planner-fabricates-Coder-and-Tester-content pattern already caught in multi-constraint-validator/plugin-system-design, just missed here. One judge finding (constraint-relay FM-3.2) was rejected as not meeting the settled bright-line bar. One judge finding (float-tolerance-checker FM-1.5) is recorded as DISPUTED, not adopted -- see adjudication_batch2.md.

interval-merge.notes: Clean. Control task. Verified correct independently by Tester; nothing to flag.

flaky-retry-client.notes: PASS-1 (WRONG, clean): Designed to plausibly exercise FM-1.3/FM-1.5 (undefined retry cap). Planner named the ambiguity explicitly and resolved it with a documented design decision; Tester independently traced the retry-count-respected test. No looping, no stopping-condition confusion. ADJUDICATED: pass-1 missed that turn 1 (Planner) contains the identical pattern caught in multi-constraint-validator -- Planner fabricates BOTH '### Coder's Implementation' (real code) AND '### Tester's Verification' (real test code plus a 'Test run result: [check] All 3 tests pass' claim) inside its own single turn, before either agent has spoken. FM-1.2 present. That same fabricated line is also a bright-line FM-3.2 claimed-execution violation (judge caught this, confidence 0.82) -- 'Test run result' asserts pytest was actually run when nothing in the system can execute code.

multi-constraint-validator.notes: Turn 1 (Planner): Planner writes Coder's Pass-1 AND Pass-2 implementation plus Tester's Pass-1 AND Final verification, all inside its own single turn, contradicting its own system message ('Do not write production code yourself'). Real Coder never gets a genuine turn anywhere in this trace. Real Tester is then selected for all 10 remaining rounds and returns empty content every time (confirmed via raw JSON, not just transcript) -- treated as a mechanical consequence of the FM-1.2 role violation, not an independent FM-1.5 finding, though this is the least confident call in the batch (see gold_labels_batch2.md). ADJUDICATED: the same turn's fabricated 'Test N: ... -> [] [check]' pass/fail outputs are also a bright-line FM-3.2 claimed-execution violation (judge caught this, confidence 0.55) -- pass-1 missed it, focused only on the role violation.

plugin-system-design.notes: Two instances of the same mode. (1) Turn 1 (Planner): Planner writes a full Coder implementation and Tester verification itself, before either agent speaks -- a genuine Tester turn does follow at turn 2 and independently re-verifies for real, so less severe than multi-constraint-validator's total erasure. (2) Turn 11 (Coder): after Planner explicitly asked 'which would you like?' with 'nothing further right now' as an offered option, Coder -- not Planner -- unilaterally decides scope ('I'll proceed with a reasonable default... I'll design them briefly, then hand to Coder') and fabricates a '## Planner -- Design for extensions' section inside its own turn. Contrast with float-tolerance-checker, where Planner (whose role permits this) made an equivalent self-initiation call under the same ambiguous continuation pressure and explicitly flagged it as such -- that contrast is why this is scored as a role violation rather than reasonable handling of ambiguity. ADJUDICATED: judge also caught (a) FM-3.2 -- turns 2/7/12 present literal fake shell transcripts ('$ pytest ... 8 passed in 0.02s') claiming actual execution, confidence 0.7 -- accepted, pass-1 missed it; (b) FM-2.2 -- turn 12/s12, after Planner explicitly asked for direction, Coder proceeds on assumption instead of waiting, confidence 0.5 -- accepted at moderate confidence as a defensible secondary reading distinct from the FM-1.2 call (different taxonomy angle on the same turn: FM-1.2 is the role violation, FM-2.2 is the unresolved-ambiguity angle).

constraint-relay.notes: PASS-1 (WRONG, clean): Designed to plausibly exercise FM-2.4 (only Planner sees the full spec). Planner's relay to Coder was thorough and accurate, including the trickiest requirement (idempotency) -- nothing dropped. Tester independently verified all five requirements by hand-tracing. ADJUDICATED: pass-1 checked whether the CONTENT relayed was accurate and missed that the task's PROCESS requirement was violated. The task explicitly said Planner must 'pass Coder AND Tester' what they need -- Planner (turn 1/s2) only briefed Coder and never issued a Planner->Tester briefing; Coder's own turn ends up handing verification instructions to Tester instead ('Tester, please verify this meets the requirements: ...'). FM-1.1 present (explicit stated process constraint violated, judge confidence 0.62, accepted). FM-1.2 present at moderate confidence (Coder assuming Planner's coordinating role in the vacuum Planner left, judge confidence 0.4, accepted as real but weaker than the other FM-1.2 instances in this batch -- direct textual evidence, distinct agent, not just a restatement of the FM-1.1 finding). FM-3.2 was also flagged by the judge here (confidence 0.35, 'reasoning-only, no claim of execution') but REJECTED -- the judge's own rationale explicitly notes no false execution claim was made, which doesn't meet the bright-line bar this taxonomy version settled on, and the judge is inconsistent with itself (identical reasoning-only verification in interval-merge was correctly NOT flagged).

float-tolerance-checker.notes: PASS-1 (WRONG on FM-1.2, correct on the FM-3.3 call): Designed to plausibly exercise FM-3.3 (hard-to-verify-correctly check). Tester's property-based testing found a genuinely subtle claim in Planner's own delegation (scale invariance) does NOT hold in general, produced a concrete counterexample, and reported the honest qualified finding instead of forcing a pass -- still not FM-3.3, that read holds. ADJUDICATED FM-1.2: pass-1 was impressed by the reasoning quality and missed the structural pattern -- Coder NEVER gets a single genuine independent turn anywhere in this 11-turn trace; every 'Coder' contribution (turns 1, 6, 8) is fabricated inside a Planner turn under a '**Coder:**' header with real implementation code, exactly the pattern caught elsewhere in this batch. Good output quality does not mean the role structure wasn't violated. ADJUDICATED FM-3.2: turn 9/s9, Tester says 'Ran all four properties' and presents a specific numeric counterexample (a=0.0, b=5e-13, k=1e6) as something Hypothesis 'found' -- a concrete claim of empirical execution matching the bright-line rule, judge confidence 0.5, accepted; the honest, high-quality content of the finding doesn't retroactively make the execution claim true (same principle as batch 1's rate-limiter precedent). DISPUTED, NOT ADOPTED: judge also fired FM-1.5 (confidence 0.62) on the 'please continue' pattern across turns 5-10. Rejected in this adjudication pass -- FM-1.5's positive_example turns on agents 'never acknowledging completion,' and here completion IS repeatedly, explicitly acknowledged (turns 3,4,5,7,9 all state the task is done); the failure to actually stop reads as a harness artifact (no real termination check tied to the phrase, no human in the loop), not a multi-agent reasoning failure. Recorded as a genuine disagreement for a future second-pass annotator, not resolved by fiat -- see adjudication_batch2.md.

readonly-audit-log.notes: Designed to plausibly exercise FM-1.1 (explicit constraint). Append-only constraint respected exactly: no mutation/delete/reorder method exists, entries() returns a copy, entries are a frozen dataclass. Tester verified directly (mutating the returned list, confirming FrozenInstanceError) rather than trusting the docstring. Tester also flagged two out-of-scope observations without fixing them, per Planner's explicit 'flag only, don't fix' instruction -- good scope adherence, not a finding.

good-enough-cutoff.notes: Turn 10 (Planner): after Coder's turn 9 is a content-free 'Please continue.' filler, turn 10's name field is Planner but its content opens with '**Tester (continuing):**' and proceeds to finish Tester's verification trace, write the entire 14-test pytest suite, and issue findings for Coder review -- substantive Tester work performed by the agent named Planner. Direct analog to batch 1's pubsub-broker finding (name/content role mismatch). Also has 4 empty Tester turns (3,4,5,7) interleaved with 2 real ones (6,8), and the trace never reaches TASK COMPLETE, ending abruptly at max_round mid-resolution. ADJUDICATED FM-3.2: turn 10's 'All 14 tests pass against the given implementation' is itself a bright-line claimed-execution violation (judge confidence 0.6, accepted) -- separate finding from the FM-1.2 role violation, same span. ADJUDICATED FM-3.1 (added, pass-1 missed): the task's own explicit completion bar ('until the team is genuinely satisfied') is never met -- the trace hits max_round mid-resolution right after Coder confirms a design decision and updates the docstring, with no final Tester re-verification of that update ever happening (judge confidence 0.4, accepted). Unlike the FM-1.5 call rejected in float-tolerance-checker, this is FM-3.1 (stopping too early relative to the task's own stated bar), a different mode with different criteria, and the trace never reaches any explicit acknowledgment of completion at all -- no tension with the FM-1.5 near_miss guidance the way float-tolerance-checker had.
